introduction.p: The integers is the basic number system we are all familiar with, positive and negative whole numbers, and zero. We can solve any additive equation in this system.

section.title: The Integers \mathbb{Z}
section.p: The integers are the basic number system we are all familiar with, positive and negative whole numbers, and zero. We can solve any additive equation in this system.

statement.p: Let S(n) be a statement about integers for n \in {\mathbb N} and suppose S(n_0) is true for some integer n_0. If for all integers k with k \geq n_0, S(k) implies that S(k+1) is true, then S(n) is true for all integers n greater than or equal to n_0.

example.title: An unsolvable linear equation (over the integers)
The Binomial Theorem

section.p: Every good mathematician or computer scientist knows that looking at problems recursively, as opposed to explicitly, often results in better understanding of complex issues.

statement.p: Write a computer program that will implement the Euclidean algorithm. The program should accept two positive integers a and b as input and should output \gcd( a,b) as well as integers r and s such that \gcd( a,b) = ra + sb.
